specs.rationale: Index and architecture overview of all SDD specs, provides context for the generate subsystem structure and spec organization patterns essential for understanding codegen integration
key_requirements: Spec format priority (OpenRPC > JSON Schema > Mermaid)
Phase flow from Init through Merge
Separation between interfaces and logic layers
Generate subsystem architecture overview

specs.rationale: Defines CLI commands that invoke SDD workflow including artifact creation and context-reference, which are entry points for code generation and test generation invocation
key_requirements: Core workflow subcommands under cclab sdd (context-reference, artifact create, etc.)
JSON file input support via --json-file flag
State machine interoperability with STATE.yaml
CliModule trait and distributed_slice registration
Support for complex payload inputs

specs.rationale: Core architecture for code generation system. Defines unified internal representation (JSON Schema basis), template-based generation, pluggable generators (Axum, FastAPI, Express), and test generation - directly implements #932 last-mile codegen requirements
key_requirements: Unified internal representation based on JSON Schema
Template-based generation engine (Tera/MiniJinja)
Pluggable generators for different frameworks/targets
Pre-generation spec validation
Test generation support via TestGenerator component

specs.rationale: Defines SpecIR enum that unifies all spec types (ApiSpec, SequencePlus, FlowchartPlus, ClassPlus, ErdPlus, RequirementPlus) as universal input format for code generators - foundational for #932 consuming SpecIR
key_requirements: SpecIR enum variants for all 6 spec types
SpecIR metadata (source file path, spec group, spec ID, tags)
From<T> implementations from Generate types
Public API export from cclab-sdd lib.rs
Serialization support for MCP transport

specs.rationale: Defines YAML manifest schema for SpecIR with Kubernetes-style envelope (apiVersion, kind, metadata, spec) and kind registry - the serialization contract for SpecIR passed to generators
key_requirements: Standard envelope structure (apiVersion, kind, metadata, spec)
Kind registry (Api, FlowchartPlus, SequencePlus, ClassPlus, ErdPlus, RequirementPlus)
Strict YAML serialization with unknown field rejection
Language-agnostic interface for code generators

specs.rationale: Axum (Rust) code generator implementation - one of two first codegen targets per Q1. Transforms SpecIR to Rust context with type mapping, generates models/handlers/router configuration
key_requirements: Generator interface accepting Spec + output directory
Context transformation to Rust-specific types (i32, String, Option<T>, Vec<T>)
Model generation with Serde derives
Router and handler generation with Axum config
Error handling with standardized GeneratorError

specs.rationale: FastAPI (Python) code generator implementation - one of two first codegen targets per Q1. Maps IR types to Pydantic models, renders routes and project layout with deterministic output
key_requirements: Input mapping from schema IR to FastAPI context
Template rendering with TemplateEngine
Standard FastAPI project layout generation
Type mapping to Pydantic (str, int, float, bool, List, BaseModel)
Deterministic output ordering, structured error reporting

specs.rationale: Test generation integration spec defining framework-specific test artifact production and cclab-probe adapter integration - directly implements #933 test-generation-from-requirementplus requirements
key_requirements: Test artifact generation (fixtures, client helpers, test cases) for FastAPI, Express, Axum
Probe adapter packaging with metadata (endpoints, expected responses, runtime config)
Deterministic test file naming and content ordering
Structured error reporting (generator type, template name, human-readable cause)

specs.rationale: Enhanced Requirement+ spec supporting SysML v1.6 types and verification methods - the source spec format for test generation in #933 (test-generation-from-requirementplus-spec)
key_requirements: SysML v1.6 type support (functionalRequirement, interfaceRequirement, performanceRequirement, etc.)
Risk levels (Low, Medium, High) and verification methods (Analysis, Inspection, Test, Demonstration)
Relationship types (satisfies, verifies, refines, traces, contains, copies, derives)
YAML frontmatter validation for all new types

specs.rationale: Generator contract principle - specs describe WHAT, generators decide HOW. Defines detailed mappings for SequencePlus→Code and RequirementPlus→Tests - critical for understanding codegen architecture and inference rules
key_requirements: Generator responsibilities (framework-specific code from agnostic specs)
Spec-to-code mappings (API schema → Models, Sequence+ → Function signatures, Requirement+ → Tests)
Inference rules (auto-infer patterns from spec semantics)
SequencePlus macro/micro level code generation
RequirementPlus N:M test generation (N Requirements → N Test Classes, N Scenarios → N Functions)

specs.rationale: Generation subsystem overview documenting architecture of generate/ specs including codegen-system, spec-validator, template-engine, test-generation - provides context on spec IR and spec-to-code subsystem
key_requirements: Code generation and template library organization
Spec IR contract and schema specifications
Code generator contract definition
Generator implementations (Axum, FastAPI, Express)
Integration architecture

notes: Reference context for 'specir-and-test-codegen' group (issues #933 feat-sdd-test-generation-from-requirementplus-spec, #932 feat-sdd-codegen-last-mile-consume-specir-to-gener). Focus: SpecIR contract as universal input format, code generation for Axum/FastAPI with templates, test generation from Requirement+ specs with deterministic output.
